message:
fix(core): CREATE INDEX backfills existing rows, enforce UNIQUE index constraints

CREATE INDEX and CREATE UNIQUE INDEX now scan all existing table rows and
insert index keys via a compiled VDBE program. This ensures:
- Indexes created on populated tables contain all existing data
- UNIQUE INDEX creation fails if existing data has duplicates
- Subsequent INSERTs correctly detect uniqueness violations against
  pre-existing rows

Also add index-level UNIQUE enforcement in execute_insert_or_conflict
fallback path (checks table_schema.indexes in addition to column-level
unique flags).

Add 4 new probe tests: views/indexes/schema, transaction semantics,
multi-join/groupby (3-table), self-joins.

1565 tests passed, 0 failures. Clippy + fmt clean.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
